title: WaveFlow plugin registry
description: Curated catalogue of installable WaveFlow plugins. The desktop app fetches this file, shows the entries in its in-app store, and installs the pinned release of each plugin after verifying its blake3.

schema_version.description: Bumped only on a breaking change to this schema. The app refuses a registry whose schema_version it doesn't understand.

id.description: Stable plugin id. MUST equal the install-dir name and the manifest.toml [plugin] id — the app refuses a mismatch.

description.description: One or two sentences shown on the store card.

repo.description: GitHub owner/name hosting the plugin's releases. The pinned release asset is downloaded from this repo.

world.description: The WIT world the plugin exports. Must match manifest.toml [plugin] world.

version.description: The published semver the registry pins. Updating a plugin = bump this + blake3 in one commit here (the curation gate).

blake3.description: blake3 hex of the plugin.wasm shipped in the pinned release. The app verifies the downloaded wasm against this before loading — the registry, not the release, is the trusted source of truth.

asset.description: Release asset filename. Defaults to <id>-v<version>.zip when omitted.

permissions.description: Mirror of manifest.toml [permissions]. Shown to the user before install AND enforced at runtime by the host sandbox (unlike omniget, where capabilities are cosmetic).

http.description: Allowlisted outbound hostnames. Empty = no network.

storage_read.description: May read its bundled sidecar assets.

storage_state.description: May persist to its private scratch store (10 MB quota).

official.description: First-party plugin maintained by the WaveFlow team. The store badges these; community plugins carry an 'unverified' hint.

min_app_version.description: Minimum WaveFlow version required. The store greys out install below this.
